_comment: Built by hand from job_description.docx. Weights are our judgment call, justified in challenge_analysis.md, not learned -- there's no ground truth to fit them against before submission. We treat this file as the single place that encodes 'what the JD means', so the scoring code in src/features.py never re-derives JD logic; rerun phase4_job_repr.py and review the diff if the JD wording changes.
role: Senior AI Engineer - Founding Team, Redrob AI

experience_band.note: JD explicitly says this is a guideline, not a cutoff: 'we'll seriously consider candidates outside the band if other signals are strong'. Used as a soft scoring curve, not a filter.

title_tiers._comment: Tier 5 = near-exact title match. We score by tier, not by string equality, because the JD itself argues against keyword title matching (the Backend-Engineer-with-Kaggle-side-projects case in our sample data is exactly the kind of candidate a pure title filter would miss).

penalized_titles._comment: JD explicitly disqualifies these unless paired with strong counter-evidence in career_history.
penalized_titles.AI Research Engineer: pure-research signal in title; JD disqualifies academic/research-only without production deployment
penalized_titles.Junior ML Engineer: JD targets 5-9 yrs senior band; junior title is a strong band mismatch, not disqualifying alone
penalized_titles.Computer Vision Engineer: JD explicitly deprioritizes CV/speech/robotics without NLP/IR exposure

service_only_career.note: JD: fine if currently at one of these but has prior product-company experience -- penalty only applies when 100% of career_history is at these firms.

framework_only.rule: penalize if LangChain appears and recent (<=12mo experience implied) AND no pre-LLM-era production ML evidence (no embeddings/retrieval/ranking skill with duration_months > 24)

cv_speech_without_nlp_ir.rule: title or skills dominated by Computer Vision / Speech / Robotics with no Information Retrieval, Embeddings, Ranking, or NLP skill present

title_chaser.rule: 3+ employers in career_history with average tenure < 18 months and escalating seniority words in title

location_preference.note: JD: 'Outside India: case-by-case, but we don't sponsor work visas.' Treated as a moderate scoring penalty, not a hard filter -- a strong non-India candidate with willing_to_relocate=true should not be zeroed out.

notice_period_curve.note: JD: 'we'd love sub-30-day notice... 30+ day notice candidates are still in scope but the bar gets higher.'

composite_weights._comment: Matches the formula specified in the challenge brief. See metrics_report.md section 'weight ablation' for what happens if these are changed -- semantic and retrieval_skill_match dominate, which is appropriate for a JD this specific about required skills.
